item.title: Re: -1
item.content:encoded:
it seem like the anomaly could be falsify and the two O5 Actually plan it out (again they actually discussed it in private), and could be O5-12 check the SCP list to see what he can do and this SCP in question could be the solution since it could fly over the other O5 radar. O5-3 could just be faking having such infection (since again they are trying to fool the O5 which could be difficult to do) and she want to be out of monitoring but there also the problem of the Foundation to monitor O5-3 TBH.

hope this get expanded via tales tho. since I want to see if they could pulled a different thing to get O5-3 out, I mean there a lot of thing they can try to pull tho then again trying to pull something different could start to make the O5 questioning about it. But IDK why its "neutralized" since they try to play it out the lie for a few more months or years until the O5 think it not longer a proper use.
I liked this—it was easy to follow and I didn't see the twist coming until the very end. As an anomaly however, it feels like it would be better as a tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Brilliant, tragic, incredibly true to what I imagine the O5 to be. Great work. Enthusiastic +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They should throw 05-3 a retirement party. I think that would make a cool tale

Have not seen anything formatted like this, I absolutely love it and the eyecandy.

The story of Mary and the SCP it self is great. I refuse to believe that the Foundation would let someone at this high of a level continue to work in the foundation when affected by a anomaly.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There is an "SCP-XXXX" in the first addendum.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a good concept, a an O5 trying to help his friend escape the O5 life, but I just refuse to believe that the O5s would even consider allowing someone that they believe to be infected with an anomalous degenerative condition to continue working in the top levels of the Foundation. That being said, it is very well-written, but I can't get around that one thing, so no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ general stuff
- hide

A big plus about this is the "O5 wanting to retire and actually be happy in a life unaffiliated with the Foundation" angle here. It's kinda open for exploration conceptually, and leaves room for some really great emotional punches, parallel to some people's desire to leave work and live a life they consider to be a "happy" one.

Though, I am a tad unsure why the other O5s didn't really arrange like, an advanced surgical procedure to check if she really had a parasitic infection? Hmm. I guess this statement:

During the procedure, Richard Williams expired, and Dr. Eliot [DATA EXPUNGED].

Kinda reflects some of the O5s' uncertainty in doing anything about O5-3 since, well, we don't know what would happen if she did undergo such a procedure. But idk, it still stuck out a bit to me.

I kinda liked the interview; it was gut-wrenching to me, especially with how O5-3 (or Dr. Eliot during that time) behaved like a mother to Rick. The ending/twist was a little impactful, though I wasn't sure if it was enough punch; us not seeing O5-3's response to O5-12 saying that he's sorry for not being able to fulfill her wish kinda dampened the big sad a little? Idk, I think it's really a personal preference when it comes to this, but I was kinda hoping for O5-3 to respond back to 12 and say something like "it's ok, and thank you for the effort anyways; Lucy can wait for a few more years, I guess", maybe?

Hmm. There were definitely some emotional hitters here and there, and the angle taken here is pretty unique, I think, but I wasn't extremely sure if it managed to hit me sufficiently overall. Novote.

+ very nitpicky stuff
- hide

SCP-5095 been assumed to be neutralized.

Maybe "SCP-5095 has been assumed…"?

SCP-5095 is a anomalous

I think "an" fits better here than "a"?

of the family Taeniidae

I was thinking swapping the positions of "family" and "Taeniidae" would fit better?

consuming rational thought

Hmm. I'm a little iffy on an organism being able to "eat" rational thought here; I was thinking maybe it eats/destroys neurons affiliated with cognitive functions would work slightly better here? Idk.

In the interview, the action cues say "Williams" while Dr. Eliot addressed Richard by "Rick". I was wondering if going all the way with "Richard" instead of "Williams" would work better? Idk, it personally felt a little troubling to read back to see if I'm seeing the wrong person.

I really liked this, it packed several emotional punches and I think writers who strive to make dramatic documents should take note.

Also very minor edit request; for the Suspected infection dates you gave each date a comma except for Arnold Witts who is just "August 1938" while the others are "December, 1938" and "April, 1938". I would remove the commas as those don't need to be there.

So, the Overseers, upon learning that one of their own, someone who makes numerous important decisions in their daily work, has an anomalous parasite residing in their head that impairs cognitive function. And they vote to do nothing about it.

Sure, they're good so far. But:

On 5/30/1949, Dr. Mary Eliot attempted to remove an SCP-5095 instance from within a live victim, Richard Williams. During the procedure, Richard Williams expired, and Dr. Eliot [DATA EXPUNGED].

I know that data shouldn't be expunged for us, but the old files are corrupted and I'm unable to recover the full clearance files. It's also possible the O5-3 expunged this data herself. — O5-12

There's actual evidence that O5-3 may be actively hiding information on their condition from the other overseers. Who knows how they actually are, if information might be out there that says otherwise? That merits an interrogation/medical check up at the very least.

item.title: Wow
item.content:encoded:
Well this just shows that "Cold not Cruel" applies to everyone, even the O5s. Poor Mary

+1

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Hi! It looks like I finally did a collab thing. Yay?

The original idea is Mew's, but I hopped on it halfway through after critting it and accidentally became a coauthor somehow. Together, we fleshed out the idea into what it is now, and I have to say, this was an absolutely fantastic experience!

Massive thanks to the awesome Mew-ltiverse for letting me write this up with her, and another huge thanks to all the various critters for this, who will be listed by Mew because I was too lazy to ask around IRC ( っ • — •)っ

Lastly, thanks to you for reading, and I hope you enjoyed!

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded: Thanks to DrAkimoto, Oboebandgeek99, cybersqyd and anyone else who looked at this for help! This was waaaaaaaay different initially. And HUGE thanks to Greyve, my co-author! They originally just critiqued it, but ended up helping me so much I offered it be a collab! This experimental piece was fun to work on! Till next time!
